title: EB-Lab · Lab manual
text:
Preface
EB-Lab (Estela Blanco, together with the ClimChange-NewbornHealth GitHub organization) focuses on environmental exposure and perinatal health in Chile and comparable settings. This manual summarizes how we collaborate, version analyses, and publish code so work stays reviewable and reproducible.
It draws inspiration from the Benjamin-Chung lab manual and modular resources such as Yale DSDE’s Book of Workshops.

title: EB-Lab · Lab manual
text:
How to use this manual
Each chapter is a quick decision or checklist before git push:

Who should read it and when.
Minimum standards before independent work.
Links to guidelines and reference repositories.

For the Spanish edition, use Español in the top bar.

title: EB-Lab · Lab manual
text:
Scope






ImportantLimits



This manual does not replace institutional protocols (ethics, consent, administrative confidentiality, etc.). It does set minimum technical standards: clear README, MIT license, large data off-repo, relative paths.

title: Welcome to the team
text:
Mission
EB-Lab works with projects under ClimChange-NewbornHealth. The public lab site is estelablanco.cl.
We build reproducible evidence on how climate and environmental stressors relate to early neonatal outcomes, alongside local and international partners. We combine epidemiology, public spatial/climate data when appropriate, and transparent statistical modeling.

title: Welcome to the team
text:
Coordination



Role
Email
Notes

Scientific questions and priorities.


Reproducibility · repository management
jdconejeros@uc.cl
Research collaborator – repository manager (per org guidelines).









TipORCID



Estela Blanco · José Daniel Conejeros.


Useful links: ClimChange-NewbornHealth · Lab website.

Minimum expectations

Agree on shared data scope (time window, population filters, updates) before irreversible production code on large tables.
Prefer clear async updates when distributed: summary + findings + concrete questions.
Credit dependencies: if you reuse someone’s function in publishable work, cite them in code and outputs.

title: Welcome to the team
text:
Relation to official guidelines
Folder structure, README, license, and external data requirements are maintained in Lineamientos_colaboradores. The manual summarizes but does not replace them.

title: Lab culture
text: Before autonomous work

title: Lab culture
text:
Intellectual honesty about design limits; avoid overstating identification.
Respect and collaboration even in fast debate.
Progressive transparency — version reproducible decisions unless legally restricted.


Day-to-day communication

Post short status if parallel tasks stall (>48h) and someone is waiting.
Prefer reproducible artifacts (tables, figures on disk) over one-off screenshots.
Be specific in requests (“validate date logic before merge?” not “can you look at everything?”).

title: Lab culture
text:
Resolving scientific/technical disagreements







Situation
Suggested flow




Variable transform dispute
Open an issue + minimal comparison branch compare/<topic>.


Authorship / release priority
Document roles and author order in the planning issue tied to the product.


Ethics or sensitive data uncertainty
Pause public release; escalate via agreed institutional channels.

title: Lab culture
text:
Inclusive language
Use neutral or explicit wording; many readers are international even when the project is Spanish-first.

title: Practical reproducibility
text:
Principles
Per org guidelines: repositories host code; analysis tables are linked externally (e.g. Dropbox, Google Drive) with explicit access notes in the README.

title: Practical reproducibility
text:
git for history · Large data off GitHub.
Relative paths · Avoid machine-specific absolute paths in shared repos.
README · Enough story that someone understands what runs and why without running line 1.

title: Practical reproducibility
text:
What “reproducible” means here








Level
Meaning
When required




A — Internal
Script + documented environment + package versions
Thesis / internal preprints in the group.


B — Public / paper
A + public data download steps + Output_Analysis
Manuscripts, reports with DOI or active preprint.

title: Practical reproducibility
text:
Quick validation

Clean-machine rerun (container, renv, or virtualenv) by someone who did not write the pipeline.
Row parity between inputs and aggregated outputs after joins.
Comparable figures between final exports and regenerated runs.

title: Practical reproducibility
text:
Privacy
Never push direct identifiers or re-identifiable raw tables to public repos. Keep secrets in .Renviron / .env (gitignored); list required variables in the README without exposing values.

title: Repositories and conventions
text:
Common requirements (summary)
From Lineamientos_colaboradores:

title: Repositories and conventions
text:
Short names, preferably English.
Public repositories unless a contract explicitly forbids it.
Required files: README.md, .gitignore, license — MIT recommended.
Short GitHub description with scientific context.

title: Repositories and conventions
text:
Standard layout
Code/
  Process/
  Descriptive/
  Models/
Output_Analysis/
  Graphs/
  Tables/
.gitignore
README.md
LICENSE

title: Repositories and conventions
text:
README essentials
Based on the org template:

Title — ideally the paper or product title.
Metrics / funding — ANID, FONDECYT, IUP, etc.
Authors + active maintainer — email for contact.
Scientific description — background, aims, methods (abstract is enough to start).
Data — external link + script execution order.
Main results — numbered figures/tables with short notes.

title: Repositories and conventions
text:

CIIIA-ClimateBirthWeightAnalysis




Repo
Role

Heat waves & prematurity

Imputation_ClimData24

More: organization repositories.

title: Git workflows
text: Branches and commits

title: Git workflows
text:
Practice
Reason




Stable main
External collaborators should clone and run without surprise.


Feature branches (feature/heat-definitions)
Atomic review and rollback.


Imperative commit subjects
Readable history for bisecting.




Commit template
Scope: verb in infinitive (≤ 72 chars)

Optional body: why (science or tech context)

title: Git workflows
text:
Pull requests

Description — question or reproducible bug.
Checklist — README updated, relative paths, no secrets.
Reviewer — especially for Models/ and final exports.

title: Git workflows
text:
External collaboration
Forks are expected. Importing external code:

Document provenance + license in README + short code comments.
Prefer small PRs over bulk copy-paste without attribution.

title: Git workflows
text:
Repro environments (summary)



Context
Tooling




R
renv + committed lockfile


Python
Pinned requirements.txt / pyproject.toml


Mixed
Containers/Conda only if truly needed



Coding style details — tidyverse norms in R, reviews — see Code standards.

title: Code standards
text:
Languages at a glance
Some projects lean on Python for geospatial or automated ingestion; standards below apply to both.

title: Code standards
text:
Style

Short functions, verb names (compute_exposure_lag).
Comments explain “why”, not trivial dplyr steps; document epidemiologic assumptions that matter.
Shared constants first — clinical thresholds, admin cut-offs, shapefile versions in config.*.

title: Code standards
text:
Folder roles



Code/
Typical content




Process/
Ingestion, cleaning, exposures.


Descriptive/
Exploratory tables/figures (non-final).


Models/
Main estimates, sensitivities.




Outputs

Finals → Output_Analysis/Graphs & Tables.
Avoid absolute paths; use here::here() (R) or equivalent.

title: Code standards
text:
Review checklist



Check
Before merge




Formatter/linter (styler, ruff, …)
Yes


Documented runtime
Yes


New deps explained in README
Yes

title: Code standards
text:
Generative AI

Verify numbers on toy cases.
Never paste identifiable records into public prompts.
Disclose in the PR comment if a large block came from an assistant tool.

title: Quick start (checklist)
text: Day 0 — Access

title: Quick start (checklist)
text:
GitHub account with institutional email.
Org invite (if any) or planned fork.
Skim Lineamientos_colaboradores (~15 min).

title: Quick start (checklist)
text:
Day 1 — Environment

Install Quarto if you will edit this manual.
R ≥ 4.2 or project-appropriate Python + VS Code / RStudio / Positron.
Configure git name + verified email.

title: Quick start (checklist)
text:
Day 2 — First repo

Clone a reference repo (e.g. CIIIA-ClimateBirthWeightAnalysis).
Branch setup/<your-initials>.
Confirm README script order matches reality.
Run at least one Descriptive/ script on a test slice if full data is heavy.

title: Quick start (checklist)
text:
Before publishing findings

README documents external data + funding.
Output_Analysis only has agreed finals.
No secrets or identifiable tables in history.

title: Quick start (checklist)
text:
Updating this manual
quarto preview
quarto render               # Spanish → _site/
cd english && quarto render && cd ..   # English → _site/en/
On push to main, .github/workflows/publish.yml publishes gh-pages.
Public URL: https://climchange-newbornhealth.github.io/eblab-manual/
Settings → Pages should use branch gh-pages, folder / (root).

title: Contributing (projects & manual)
text:
Creating repositories
This chapter mirrors Lineamientos para colaboradores; figures are reproduced from that repo inside this manual.

title: Contributing (projects & manual)
text:
Step 1 — create the repo



Step 1 — Create repository




Step 2 — configure metadata & base files



Step 2 — Repository settings


Repositories in the EB-Lab ecosystem under ClimChange-NewbornHealth should have:

Short names, ideally English.
A concise description.
Public visibility (unless a documented exception).
README.md, .gitignore, license (MIT recommended).



Step 3 — clone locally



Step 3 — Clone URL


Use RStudio, VS Code, Positron, or any IDE that respects git + relative paths.






TipSmall PRs



Prefer short branches & reviewed PRs before merging to main, especially for Models/ and final artifacts.

title: Contributing (projects & manual)
text:
README content



README outline



Title (ideally manuscript title).
Metrics & funding (see template).
Authors incl. emails + repo maintainer.
Scientific description from the abstract where possible.
Data access links + ordered script execution.
Key results — numbered figures/tables with notes.

Template: Plantilla_README.md.

title: Contributing (projects & manual)
text:
Data stays off-repo
Repos hold code to replicate; large tables are linked externally.






WarningStorage & privacy



Uploading entire bases bloats the repo and increases disclosure risk. Keep access-controlled storage outside public git.





External data example





External data example


Example README + external data workflow: CIIIA-ClimateBirthWeightAnalysis.

title: Contributing (projects & manual)
text:
Contributing to this manual

Fork or obtain write access to eblab-manual.
Edit .qmd files / _quarto.yml as needed.
Maintain Spanish + English: run renders for both locales before PRs (quarto render + cd english && quarto render).
Open a pull request explaining the change.

Repository access questions: jdconejeros@uc.cl (Research collaborator – Repository manager).
